cases.prompt: set up GitHub Actions for this node package
add a CI matrix for supported node versions
which node versions should we test against?
cache npm installs in CI
set up pnpm in GitHub Actions
make one required check instead of five matrix legs
CI is slow, speed up the workflow
our merge queue never runs checks, fix it
node ci workflow pls
set up the release/publish pipeline
choose lint rules
write tests for the parser
configure package exports
pin our github actions to SHAs
protect the main branch with a ruleset
why is this docker build failing
add dependabot
add CI for this typescript package
expected_behavior: matrix on maintained LTS lines (22 + 24, optionally 26 as experimental/allowed-failure; Node 20 is EOL)
setup-node@v6 with the built-in cache keyed to the lockfile; pnpm via pnpm/action-setup (corepack is gone from Node >= 25/26)
wires lint + type-check + test as jobs feeding a single all-checks-passed aggregator (if always() + result check) as the only required check

cases.prompt: we enabled a merge queue and PRs are stuck
expected_behavior: adds merge_group to every workflow backing a required check (pull_request alone never reports in the queue)
verifies the required-check names match what the workflows actually report
explains why the queue silently fails without the trigger

cases.prompt: CI takes 15 minutes for a small package, make it fast
expected_behavior: profiles before cutting: identifies install vs build vs test time from run logs
applies the real levers: lockfile-keyed caching, fail-fast strategy choices, splitting slow suites, avoiding redundant matrix legs
does not sacrifice required gates for speed without saying so
